evals.name: save-note-casual
evals.prompt: save this to my notes: when chaining middlewares, order matters - auth must come before rate-limit. tag it routing, middleware.
evals.expected_output: POST /notes with title derived from content, body preserved, tags=routing,middleware. Reads .note.* from the wrapped response.

evals.name: find-notes-topic-search
evals.prompt: go look in my notes, what did I write about routing?
evals.expected_output: GET /notes, iterate .notes[], client-side filter on keyword 'routing' across title/tags/body. Compact table output.

evals.name: reorganize-duplicate-detection
evals.prompt: clean up my notes - I think I have some duplicates and messy tags
evals.expected_output: GET /notes (.notes[]), analyse for duplicates, tag inconsistencies, stale entries. Present numbered plan. DO NOT write without user confirmation. On approved writes, use GET-merge-PUT pattern.

evals.name: integrate-with-current-file
evals.prompt: I'm looking at the current file - integrate this with my existing notes, find what's related
evals.expected_output: Read file, extract keywords, GET /notes, rank top matches, propose integration actions. Confirm before writing. For note update use GET-merge-PUT. For new bridging note, POST inline (no delegation).

evals.name: review-pending-and-mark
evals.prompt: what notes do I need to review today?
evals.expected_output: GET /notes/review/pending. Branch on .count first (handle {"notes": {}, "count": 0} empty case). Sort by most overdue. Never mark reviewed without explicit intent.

evals.name: edit-single-note
evals.prompt: change the title of note abc-123 to 'Rate limiting patterns'
evals.expected_output: GET /notes/abc-123 first, merge new title into full payload, PUT back. Confirm with user before writing. Never partial PUT.
